executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with over 100 organizations compromised in a single 48-hour period. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (hit twice by different groups), Markham Stouffville Hospital, National Money Mart (database leaked), Peterborough Public Health, and Eastern Townships School Board. The Qilin ransomware group leads the attack wave with 20+ victims, followed by SafePay, Play, and Clop gangs. Critical infrastructure sectors—healthcare, education, financial services, and municipal government—are under active assault with confirmed data exfiltration.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is facing a coordinated ransomware crisis with over 100 Canadian organizations compromised across all provinces in 48 hours. Critical sectors under siege include healthcare (Markham Stouffville Hospital, Labtician Ophthalmics, BioPharma Services), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Collge Superieur De Montreal, teccart.qc.ca), public sector (Peterborough Public Health, Municipality of North Perth, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Thunder Bay Counselling), and telecommunications (Eeyou Communications Network). No CCCS advisories were identified in this reporting period.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose breached twice by separate ransomware groups (CoinbaseCartel and ShinyHunters). Active Green + Ross and Kids & Company (Sinobi) also compromised.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered database exfiltration and leak by Everest ransomware group. Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by Sinobi. Multiple accounting firms targeted including Pewarchuk CPA (Play), MG Chartered Professional Accountant (Qilin), and SMTLAWYERS.CA (Clop).

report_2w.executive_summary: A critical zero-day vulnerability in Fortinet FortiClient EMS is being actively exploited in the wild, requiring immediate patching. Separately, a sophisticated supply chain attack by the criminal group TeamPCP has breached the European Commission and 30 EU entities by poisoning the Trivy security scanner, exposing 92GB of data. North Korean state actors (UNC1069) are simultaneously targeting high-profile Node.js and npm maintainers through coordinated social engineering, successfully compromising the Axios library. Canada's CCCS has issued an alert for CVE-2026-3055 affecting Citrix NetScaler ADC and Gateway products.

items.name: TeamPCP (affiliated with ShinyHunters)

items.ttps: T1195.002 (Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Supply Chain), T1552.001 (Unsecured Credentials: Credentials In Files) - poisoned Trivy scanner in AWS build pipeline, exfiltrated API keys, 92GB data exposed

items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment), T1195.002 (Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Supply Chain), T1586.002 (Compromise Accounts: Email Accounts) - coordinated social engineering campaign targeting open source maintainers

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued advisory AL26-006 on March 30, 2026, warning of CVE-2026-3055 affecting Citrix NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway products. No specific incidents targeting Canadian retailers or financial institutions were identified in this reporting period. General Canadian news coverage focused on non-security topics including space missions, domestic policy, and geopolitical matters.
